lenses.guidance: vv 1-4's seven-lamp lampstand, kept burning continually, echoes across Scripture into Zech 4:2-6's vision and Rev 1:12-13's seven golden lampstands. The threaded image of unfading light runs from tabernacle to throne room.

vv 5-9's bread of the Presence — twelve loaves, replaced sabbath after sabbath — anticipates Christ's 'I am the bread of life' (Jn 6:35). The pattern: bread perpetually before God, fulfilled in the bread that feeds eternally.

vv 10-23's blasphemy case interrupts cult instructions with narrative. The Hebrew naqab (v 11) extends from 'to pierce' to 'to pronounce a name in curse' — verbal violation of the holy Name. The literal sense roots the figurative.
